title: Frizzened Kenyan Mentaiko Pilaf
description: A briny and light weeknight dish featuring mentaiko in a molcajete for a unique twist on traditional kenyan cuisine.

ingredients.name: Molcajete
ingredients.notes: Ensure it is clean and dry.

ingredients.name: 1 cup of rice
ingredients.notes: Aromatic long-grain or any variety you prefer.

ingredients.name: 1 can (400g) of anchovy fillets in oil
ingredients.notes: Available at specialty food stores or online.

ingredients.name: 50 grams of mentaiko (smoked salmon roe)
ingredients.notes: Premium quality, optional but recommended for bold taste.

ingredients.name: Vegetable oil for seasoning the molcajete (optional)

instructions: Wipe your molcajete clean and dry, ensuring no previous residue remains.
Add a small amount of vegetable oil to the bottom of the molcajete for non-stick performance if needed.
Combine rice and curry powder in the molcajete. Mix well until evenly distributed.

Add anchovy fillets (with their oil), minced garlic, lemon juice, mentaiko, salt, pepper, and lime juice to the molcajete. Mix all ingredients together vigorously until a paste-like consistency is achieved.
Gradually add hot water to form a pilaf base, stirring continuously with wooden utensils or mortar pestle while adding water, ensuring no lumps remain.
Cook for about 10 minutes over medium heat, allowing the mixture to simmer gently and absorb flavors.
Once cooked, continue stirring until the pilaf has thickened slightly but remains soft enough to eat. Serve hot with a side of butter or a light drizzle of oil for added richness.
